title: CEO Commentary on Loan Condition - As of March 31, 2026
content:
DATA CHECK: The source table lists 'ALL OTHER NON-COMMERCIAL' with a dollar figure of $81 (in thousands) and a % of Total of 0.0%. However, the total loans figure is $6,657,942 (in thousands). A value of $81 represents approximately 0.001% of the total, not 0.0%, but given the rounding convention in financial reporting where values below a certain threshold are often rounded to zero for percentage display, this discrepancy is noted as a formatting artifact rather than an error requiring correction. The commentary will use the literal dollar figure ($81) and omit it from the narrative if its % contribution is negligible or inconsistent with the '0.0%' label.

CEO Commentary on Loan Condition - As of March 31, 2026
CAMPUS's loan portfolio remains actively managed and appropriately reserved, though overall loan balances continued to decline during the period. Total loans of $6.7 million remain concentrated in vehicle lending (new and used) and unsecured lines, which continue to represent the primary sources of both earnings and credit risk. While delinquencies increased modestly from the previous year-end, overall portfolio risk remains manageable relative to the credit union's strong capital position and reserve coverage. Management continues to focus on maintaining credit quality through disciplined underwriting and portfolio monitoring.

Portfolio Composition
CAMPUS's loan portfolio is primarily concentrated in vehicle lending segments. As of March 31, 2026, new vehicle loans totaled $1.58 billion (23.7%) and used vehicle loans totaled $1.43 billion (21.4%), together representing approximately 45% of the total portfolio.

All other non-commercial real estate loans totaled $0.08 million (negligible), all other secured non-real estate loans totaled $25.2 million (3.9%), and unsecured credit card lines totaled $12.9 million (1.9%). Commercial loan segments, including first lien real estate ($1.87 billion) and junior lien real estate ($0.54 billion), comprised the remaining balance of approximately 56%.

Overall loan balances declined slightly from the previous year-end, reflecting a cautious lending environment amidst economic headwinds affecting consumer demand for non-essential purchases like vehicles.

Delinquency & Credit Quality
Credit performance remains manageable despite elevated delinquency levels within certain unsecured and vehicle segments. Total delinquent loans increased to approximately $675K, representing a 0.2% increase from the previous year-end. The largest delinquency exposure remains in used vehicle loans ($143M) followed by new vehicle loans ($158M), with smaller exposures in unsecured credit cards and other non-commercial segments.

The concentration of delinquencies within vehicle lending reflects broader industry trends affecting consumer auto financing, while management continues to monitor migration trends and collection performance to limit future credit deterioration.
